name: licm_unhoist
title: LICM unhoist via regfix delete-preheader + insert-inline
summary: When target keeps a magic-constant lui+ori inline (with the ori filling a `lw` delay slot) and our variant hoists it to a t-reg in the loop preheader, bridge with regfix delete + insert + register cascade.
proof: SetCurrentCursor (commit 22c1cc0) -- 7-rule recipe brought it to byte match.

structural: Target has `lui $X, 0xNNNN` followed by `lw ...`, with `ori $X,$X,0xNNNN` filling the delay slot of the lw
Our pipeline output puts the lui+ori in a loop preheader, with the lw alone in the body
The const value 0xNNNN0000 + 0xNNNN appears inline at exactly one site in target

rule_skeleton.comment: cascade-rename: free up the t-reg now unused

notes: Use insert_after with `idx=B_LW` to place the ori in the lw's delay slot (regfix interprets insert_after as 'right after this index').
After deletion, downstream indices shift -- always run `dump_text_indices.py` to reconfirm before adding the cascade swap.
The cascade swap renames the now-orphan preheader register to whatever target uses inline (often $t8/$t9 vs $a3).
